analysis.root_cause: The function checkSpamPatterns mixes multiple independent spam-detection heuristics (link counting, keyword scanning across multiple fields, and capitalization analysis) into one large method with several early-return conditionals.
analysis.explanation: checkSpamPatterns performs three distinct checks, each with its own data preparation and conditional logic: (1) counts URLs and returns on linkCount > 2, (2) iterates over a keyword list and checks content, author name, and author email, returning on the first match, and (3) computes an uppercase ratio and applies a threshold. These responsibilities are tightly coupled in a single method, creating a complex control flow with multiple branches and early exits. As the number of heuristics grows, the method will become harder to read, test, and extend, violating SRP/KISS and blurring separation between different detection strategies.

refactor.reasoning: The original function combines multiple independent spam-detection heuristics in one method, creating a complex method with several conditionals and early returns. Extracting each heuristic into a small helper function improves readability, testability, and separation of concerns while preserving the original control flow and behavior.

validation.comments: Extracted helper functions reduce the complexity of the main method and make each conditional check more focused, addressing both 'complex method' and 'complex conditional' concerns as a bundle. Logic and thresholds appear unchanged (same link regex/count, same keyword list and matching, same capitalization ratio/length guard, same return reasons). No new edge-case handling differences introduced.

selection.policy: best iteration: preserves_behavior (yes > uncertain > no), then higher improvement_score, then lower attempt number
